claim: Every cell in the human body is replaced every seven years.

result.claim: Every cell in the human body is replaced every seven years.
result.reasoning: The searches for propositions and chunks related to "every cell in the human body is replaced every seven years," "human cell replacement rate seven years," and "human cell turnover rate" did not yield any relevant information to support or refute the claim. The retrieved propositions and chunks discussed unrelated topics such as fetal cell microchimerism, bone marrow biopsies, and metabolic rates. There is no evidence within the provided knowledge base that addresses the specific claim of a seven-year cell replacement cycle for every cell in the human body.

evidence.text: KIFs are critical for cellular functioning.

evidence.text: The bone marrow revealed a 4% myeloblast population.

evidence.text: NK cells (CD56/16+) of fetal origin have been detected in the circulation of a large proportion of healthy women that had children.
